editor_note: The right bet was not copilot-inside-VSCode, but an editor rebuilt around the model. Tab completion, inline diffs, and cmd-K feel native rather than bolted on. By forking VS Code instead of starting from scratch, Cursor kept the muscle memory and changed the center of gravity. That is why it feels sticky instead of merely novel.
investors: Andreessen Horowitz, Thrive Capital, OpenAI Startup Fund

product_evidence.claim: Official product positioning describes Cursor: The AI-first code editor.
Tab completion, inline diffs, and cmd-K feel native rather than bolted on.

market_context.primary_user: AI builders and operators evaluating model, automation, or data workflows.
market_context.why_now: Recent source-backed funding and public product evidence make ai, code editor, developer tools worth tracking.

risks.claim: Continued tracking should verify whether the public product evidence turns into durable customer adoption beyond the currently visible surface.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
